frameworks.name: cognitive maps
frameworks.one_line: hippocampal world-centered low-dimensional representations of knowledge

frameworks.name: image spaces
frameworks.one_line: parietal self-centered spatial relationships organizing knowledge

frameworks.name: hippocampal-parietal network
frameworks.one_line: proposed joint system spanning multiple reference frames

frameworks.name: low-dimensional internal models
frameworks.one_line: general propensity of the mind to build geometric structure

core_question: How is conceptual knowledge organized across allocentric cognitive maps and egocentric image spaces in the brain?
key_assumptions: Conceptual knowledge inherits geometry from spatial representation
Spatial reference frames (world- vs self-centered) generalize to non-spatial domains
Hippocampus and parietal cortex implement distinct reference frames
key_findings: Cognitive maps map onto hippocampal formation and resemble world-centered codes
Image spaces map onto parietal cortex and resemble self-centered codes
interpretations: A hippocampal-parietal network organizes and retrieves knowledge across multiple reference frames
Both formats may be manifestations of a broader drive to build low-dimensional internal models
